desc: Tekapo is a South Island alpine-lake base known for dark skies, mountain panoramas, and easy access to scenic drives and hikes.
hls: Aoraki Mackenzie International Dark Sky Reserve stargazing
Turquoise Lake Tekapo backdrop and Church of the Good Shepherd
Mount John Observatory experiences and astro-photography access
Road-trip base for Aoraki / Mount Cook National Park day trips
Tekapo Springs hot pools, sauna, and winter snow tubing
Seasonal lupin blooms and broad South Island alpine panoramas

todo.name: Mount John guided night tour
todo.desc: Reserve an evening observatory slot and dress for cold wind even in summer shoulder nights.

todo.name: Lakeshore to Church of the Good Shepherd
todo.desc: Photograph the iconic stone church and continue around the shoreline for quieter viewpoints.

todo.name: Hooker Valley Track day trip
todo.desc: Drive to Aoraki / Mount Cook village for suspension bridges and glacier-lake views.

todo.name: Soak at Tekapo Springs
todo.desc: Book twilight pools after hikes; winter sessions are popular and can sell out.

todo.name: Ride a section of the Alps 2 Ocean trail
todo.desc: Use local bike hire or shuttle support to tackle scenic canals and open-country segments.

todo.name: Lake Pukaki and High Country viewpoints
todo.desc: Plan a half-day drive for vivid blue-water photo stops and mountain reflections.

prac.visa: New Zealand requires most visa-waiver travelers to hold an approved NZeTA before boarding; visitors also need onward travel and must meet biosecurity controls on arrival.
alerts: Winter black ice and occasional snow-chain requirements affect State Highway 8 and alpine side roads.
Peak southern summer (December to February) increases self-drive demand, with higher rates for cars and lakeside stays.
Cloud cover can cancel stargazing tours at short notice; keep a flexible backup night.
Fuel and grocery options are limited late at night compared with bigger South Island towns.

wifi.note: Town-center accommodation generally has dependable Wi-Fi, but speeds can dip at busy evening times and mobile data weakens on highways toward Mount Cook.

prac.sim: Buy an eSIM/local SIM in arrival city for best coverage and data value.
prac.safety: Use standard travel precautions and follow local weather/sea-condition guidance.
prac.fltNote: No direct London departures; typical routing is UK to Christchurch (CHC) via one or two long-haul connections, then a 3-hour drive/shuttle.
